items.title: Sam Bankman-Fried withdraws retrial motion. He believes he would not get a fair trial.
items.desc: The founder of the collapsed crypto exchange FTX said he might file for a retrial after a decision on his appeal is ruled upon.

items.title: Bitcoin's bullish momentum runs into Pentagon-backed inflation warning
items.desc: What you need to know for April 23, 2026

items.title: Blockchain Capital Seeks $700M for Two New Crypto Funds
items.desc: The venture capital firm aims to close the fundraising within five to six months, and is already deploying some of the new capital.

items.title: Bitcoin slips from near $80,000 as oil price increase weighs on risk assets
items.desc: Bitcoin dips after testing $80,000 as oil surges and traders stay bearish, even though a breakout hints the rally could accelerate on short squeezes.

items.title: ‘Timely action is critical’: Crypto industry presses Senate to act as US market structure talks drag on
items.desc: Crypto trade groups and dozens of digital asset firms urged the Senate Banking Committee to hold a markup on market structure legislation.

items.title: More than 100 crypto firms urge Senate to move on U.S. market structure bill
items.desc: Key priorities include defining clear SEC and CFTC oversight roles, protecting non-custodial developers, simplifying disclosure rules, and avoiding a patchwork of state laws.

items.title: The DAT collapse: Pantera wants Satsuma to dump its bitcoin as shares crash 99%
items.desc: Pantera Capital is urging London-listed Satsuma Technology to liquidate its remaining bitcoin holdings and return cash to shareholders.

items.title: Coinbase backer Blockchain Capital hunts $700 million for new funds
items.desc: The San Francisco-based firm is raising for its seventh early-stage fund and second growth fund, which are expected to be completed in the next five to six months.

items.title: Blockchain Capital seeks $700 million for two new funds: Bloomberg
items.desc: Blockchain Capital could complete the fundraising in five to six months, Bloomberg reported, citing a source familiar with the matter.

items.title: OpenAI appears to be poaching Coinbase’s marketing team
items.desc: Six Coinbase senior marketing executives, including the exchange's former chief marketing officer, have jumped to OpenAI in the past year and a half.

items.title: Uzbekistan launches special crypto mining zone with 10-year tax holiday
items.desc: The initiative seeks to attract investments, create new jobs, and promote the use of renewable energy resources in crypto mining.

items.title: Bitcoin's bull score index just left bear territory. There's a warning attached.
items.desc: The index has returned to neutral territory, a rare milestone that has historically marked market turning points, but not always correctly.

items.title: Cardano builder seeks smaller funding slice of $46.8 million for scaling and Bitcoin DeFi
items.desc: The engineering organization behind Cardano submitted nine proposals totaling $46.8 million for the 2026 voting cycle, down from $97.5 million last year.

items.title: MetaMask Co-founder Dan Finlay announces departure from Consensys
items.desc: Finlay highlighted the launch of Advanced Permissions, which allows dApps to execute multiple transactions on behalf of a MetaMask user.

items.title: Bitcoin loses grip near $80,000 as ETH, SOL, DOGE fade on profit-taking
items.desc: Bitcoin traded at $77,794 on Thursday morning, up 0.4% over 24 hours but well off the $79,388 high hit Wednesday evening, with ether, XRP and Solana all closing red.

items.title: SocialFi app Believe founder arrested on strangulation charges following rug pull lawsuit
items.desc: Pasternak is also facing a civil class-action lawsuit where investors of Believe's native token are accusing him of orchestrating a rug pull.

items.title: Sam Bankman-Fried withdraws new trial motion for now, says he won’t get ‘fair hearing’ from judge
items.desc: SBF withdrew his motion for a new trial for now in a letter to Judge Lewis Kaplan, adding that he didn't think he would get a "fair hearing."

items.title: US Government Runs a Bitcoin Node, But Not Mining BTC: US Admiral
items.desc: The commander of U.S. forces in the Pacific said the military is investigating Bitcoin’s ability to “secure and protect networks.”

items.title: OpenAI Launches Workspace Agents Feature in ChatGPT
items.desc: Codex-powered tools automate reports, code, Slack replies, and other workplace tasks.

items.title: Circle Proposes Emergency Rate Changes to Unstick Aave's Frozen USDC Pool
items.desc: Stablecoin issuer argues Aave's interest rate curve is failing to clear the $1.89B pool after four days at full utilization.

items.title: Elon Musk's Tesla reports unchanged bitcoin holdings, books $173 million digital asset loss
items.desc: The company's bitcoin stash remained at 11,509 BTC, worth about $880 million at bitcoin's current price of around $78,000.

items.title: AngelList's USVC Gives Investors Exposure to OpenAI, Anthropic and xAI—Starting at $500
items.desc: AngelList's new USVC fund allows non-accredited retail investors to gain exposure to major private firms like Anthropic and OpenAI.

items.title: New York Governor Hochul targets prediction markets amid growing concerns of insider betting
items.desc: New York Governor Kathy Hochul signed an executive order banning state employees from using insider information to bet on prediction markets.

items.title: New York, Illinois Ban Government Employees From Insider Trading on Prediction Markets
items.desc: The states banned government employees from trading on insider information—a growing concern as prediction markets related to politics surge in popularity.

items.title: FBI Director Kash Patel To Speak at Bitcoin 2026 Conference About “Ending the War on Bitcoin”
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine FBI Director Kash Patel To Speak at Bitcoin 2026 Conference About “Ending the War on Bitcoin” A panel titled “Code Is Free Speech: Ending The War On Bitcoin” has been announced for Bitcoin 2026, bringing together three figures from law, government, and the Bitcoin industry. The panel features FBI Director Kash Patel, Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche, and Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal. Kash Patel was confirmed as FBI Director […] This post FBI Director Kash Patel To Speak at Bitcoin 2026 Conference About “Ending the War on Bitcoin” first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Jenna Montgomery.

items.title: The U.S. Military Is Running a Bitcoin Node, Admiral Paparo Reveals
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine The U.S. Military Is Running a Bitcoin Node, Admiral Paparo Reveals The U.S. military is actively running a Bitcoin node and testing the protocol’s cryptographic architecture as a tool for securing and protecting networks, according to INDOPACOM commander Admiral Samuel Paparo. This post The U.S. Military Is Running a Bitcoin Node, Admiral Paparo Reveals first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Inside Plasma with Zaheer: The Future of Stablecoins
items.desc: Zaheer explains why stablecoins, better UX, and focused execution are key to crypto’s next phase of adoption.

items.title: “Bitcoin as Everyday Money” Event to Rally Industry Behind De Minimis Tax Framework at Bitcoin 2026
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine “Bitcoin as Everyday Money” Event to Rally Industry Behind De Minimis Tax Framework at Bitcoin 2026 Policy leaders from Block and the Bitcoin Policy Institute will headline a live event at Bitcoin 2026 calling for de minimis tax relief that treats qualifying network digital assets like Bitcoin as cash-like for small transactions, responding directly to proposals that would limit relief to stablecoins only. This post “Bitcoin as Everyday Money” Event to Rally Industry Behind De Minimis Tax Framework at Bitcoin 2026 first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Juan Galt.

items.title: The question isn't whether privacy. It's what sort of privacy
items.desc: Blockchains are going private. The only question left is what kind of privacy the industry will build, argues Schiller.

items.title: UK’s Financial Conduct Authority Leads London Crackdown on Crypto Traders
items.desc: Multi-agency raid marks shift from warnings to physical enforcement as UK regulator finds zero legally registered P2P crypto traders.

items.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Presses Senate to Pass Crypto Market Structure Legislation
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine U.S. Treasury Secretary Presses Senate to Pass Crypto Market Structure Legislation Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told a Senate panel Wednesday that passing comprehensive crypto legislation is vital to maintaining U.S. financial leadership. This post U.S. Treasury Secretary Presses Senate to Pass Crypto Market Structure Legislation first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: SparkLend Sees Over $1B in Deposits Since Kelp Exploit as Aave TVL Plunges
items.desc: Aave TVL has dropped by $10B since the Kelp attackers used the protocol to borrow $190 million in WETH, depositing unbacked rsETH.

items.title: TD Cowen flags five hurdles to crypto bill beyond stablecoin yield issue
items.desc: These include a lack of CFTC commissioners, conflicts tied to World Liberty Financial, and concerns around Iran's use of crypto payments.

items.title: Base Pushes Toward Final L2 Stage with First Independent Upgrade
items.desc: Base's Azul upgrade is currently live on testnet, with mainnet launch scheduled for mid-May.

items.title: Crypto bill nears stablecoin rewards breakthrough as talks hit ‘good spot,’ but ethics concerns set to resurface
items.desc: After months of negotiations over how a crypto bill should treat stablecoin rewards, a key negotiator says discussions are in a "good spot."

items.title: 'Axie Infinity' Gaming Network Ronin Sets Date for Ethereum Layer-2 Migration
items.desc: The Ronin gaming network will slash RON token inflation from over 20% to less than 1% while introducing automated developer rewards.

items.title: Banks seek to slow down implementation of crypto's GENIUS Act on stablecoin oversight
items.desc: U.S. banking groups argued that a number of federal agencies are moving quickly on stablecoin regulations, making it hard to understand how rules will interact.

items.title: The Protocol: Kelp DAO exploited for $292 million
items.desc: Also: DPRK hacking crypto, Aave contagion and Coinbase on quantum computing.

items.title: Russia’s Sberbank Ready to Enter Crypto Trading as Russia Moves Toward Regulation
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Russia’s Sberbank Ready to Enter Crypto Trading as Russia Moves Toward Regulation Sberbank will provide access to crypto trading once regulation and organized exchange trading begin, Senior Vice President Ruslan Vesterovsky said. items.title: Crypto Long & Short: Protecting the people building DeFi infrastructure
items.desc: In this week’s Crypto Long & Short Newsletter, Jennifer Rosenthal writes on the need to protect the people actually building DeFi infrastructure. Then, Alexis Sirkia comments on how Ethereum's L2 strategy is failing due to a fundamental design flaw.

items.title: The $292 million Kelp DAO exploit shows why crypto bridges are still one of the industry's weakest links
items.desc: The problem is structural and as long as bridges depend on complex systems with shared infrastructure and hidden trust assumptions, they will remain vulnerable.

items.title: GSR Launches Actively Managed Bitcoin, Ethereum and Solana Basket ETF on Nasdaq
items.desc: Crypto market maker GSR has launched an actively managed ETF on the Nasdaq that includes Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana.

items.title: Input Output proposes scaled-back Cardano treasury plan ahead of Leios mainnet push
items.desc: The Leios mainnet launch is at the center of the roadmap, but IO’s tracker shows the upgrade still mid-development.

items.title: American Bitcoin ($ABTC) Activates 11,000 New Bitcoin Miners, Expanding Hashrate Capacity
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine American Bitcoin ($ABTC) Activates 11,000 New Bitcoin Miners, Expanding Hashrate Capacity American Bitcoin (ABTC) announced it had energized more than 11,000 new mining machines at its Drumheller facility. This post American Bitcoin ($ABTC) Activates 11,000 New Bitcoin Miners, Expanding Hashrate Capacity first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Bitcoin tops $79,000 as crypto rally gathers steam; Circle, Coinbase, Strategy lead
items.desc: BTC pushed to an 11-week high with dynamics for a short squeeze building, one analyst noted.

items.title: Russia Advances Sweeping Crypto Bill With Provisions for 'Circumventing Sanctions'
items.desc: The legislation would license crypto exchanges and permit cross-border cryptocurrency use while maintaining domestic payment restrictions.

items.title: The London P2P sweep: UK FCA raids eight illegal peer-to-peer trading hubs
items.desc: The sites were targeted for facilitating P2P trading without required registration or anti-money laundering controls, posing a financial crime risk.

items.title: Crypto giant GSR launches its first ETF to give investors an easy way to bet on the big 3 tokens
items.desc: GSR is entering the asset management space with a new Nasdaq-listed ETF that actively manages a basket of bitcoin, ether and solana while offering investors a chance to earn staking yields.

items.title: UK FCA carries out first crackdown on illegal peer-to-peer crypto trading
items.desc: The Financial Conduct Authority said it has carried out its first operation targeting illegal peer-to-peer crypto trading in the UK.

items.title: Binance.US slashes trading fees across listed cryptocurrencies in a bid to attract users
items.desc: “American crypto traders have been paying too much for too long,” Binance.US CEO Stephen Gregory said about the move to slash fees.

items.title: Russia Advances Sweeping Crypto Regulation Bill, Legalizes Cross-Border Payments and Tightens Market Controls
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Russia Advances Sweeping Crypto Regulation Bill, Legalizes Cross-Border Payments and Tightens Market Controls Russia’s State Duma advanced a sweeping bill to regulate digital currencies, marking a major step toward formal oversight of the country’s crypto market. This post Russia Advances Sweeping Crypto Regulation Bill, Legalizes Cross-Border Payments and Tightens Market Controls first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Armed intruders force French family to transfer $820,000 in crypto: report
items.desc: Two armed men forced a French family to transfer $820,000 in crypto during a home invasion, local media reported.

items.title: 'Finding Satoshi' Makes the Case for Hal Finney, Len Sassaman as Bitcoin Co-Creators
items.desc: A new documentary asserts that Bitcoin creator Satoshi Nakamoto was actually two people: the late Hal Finney and Len Sassaman.

items.title: ‘I suspect you got to the right answer’: New Satoshi documentary makes the case Hal Finney and Len Sassaman were Bitcoin’s co-creators
items.desc: The film follows a four-year investigation led by New York Times bestselling author William D. Cohan and private investigator Tyler Maroney.

items.title: GSR enters crypto ETF field with actively managed multi-asset fund tracking Bitcoin, Ethereum and Solana
items.desc: Core3 marks GSR's first crypto ETF offering amid a period of expansion for the major market maker and the wider crypto fund sector.

items.title: WalletConnect Integrates with TradFi-Focused Chain Canton Network
items.desc: WalletConnect's ecosystem of 700 crypto wallets and 70,000 dApps now supports the enterprise- and privacy-focused blockchain network.

items.title: B2B stablecoin tech provider Infinite rolls out banking services powered by Thiel-backed Erebor Bank
items.desc: Infinite is introducing bank accounts that support traditional and crypto services like ACH, wire and stablecoin transfers.

items.title: PENGU Notches Double-Digit Gains as Bitcoin Hits $78K Amid $418M Liquidation Spree
items.desc: The crypto market outlook improved as altcoins rallied amid Bitcoin's $78,000 retest, but experts remain cautious.

items.title: Coinbase Flags Proof-of-Stake Chains Like Ethereum, Solana as Potential Quantum Risks

items.title: Strategy Now Holds $62 Billion in Bitcoin—These Are Its Biggest BTC Buys

items.title: New York Attorney General Sues Coinbase, Gemini Over Unlicensed Prediction Markets
items.desc: New York AG Letitia James filed suit against Coinbase and Gemini, alleging their prediction market platforms constitute illegal gambling operations lacking state licenses.

items.desc: Tempo also announced it's launching a Stablecoin Advisory.

items.desc: Since the freeze, ZachXBT reported that the attackers had begun moving funds from Ethereum mainnet to Bitcoin.

items.title: Privacy Boost, Sunnyside's Privacy SDK, Goes Live on Optimism Mainnet: Optimism
items.desc: Optimism's first privacy offering by core developer Sunnyside uses ZK and TEE hybrid technology to enable confidential computing for enterprises on the OP Stack.
